body: Isn't this covered by the later validation?
Would decrease the interval to 15 minutes or so.
This looks a bit weird to me ... let's discuss at the daily.

Can we calculate the exact fee based on our feeRate?
Need to multiply btcFee with vByte count. Use e.g. https://bitcoinops.org/en/tools/calc-size/ (single input/output)
One address parameter would be enough IMO.

I see a lot of code duplicated in `PurchaseStockLiquidityStrategy` and `PurchaseCryptoLiquidityStrategy` (everything except getting swap asset/amount). Did you think about merging them? It could be a could idea, especially if we might at some time also have multiple swap assets for crypto tokens.
Do these really belong to the environment?

body: Well, it is working for all other requests 😁 try reloading Thunder or restarting VS Code. Might be some caching issue.
Change metric name to `balance` instead of `combined`?
Interval is a bit small 🤣 would use 15 minutes.

Use plain SQL to get the DB balance. Something like this:
```sql
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN bt.creditDebitIndicator = 'DBIT' THEN bt.amount * -1 ELSE bt.amount END)
FROM dbo.bank_tx bt 
INNER JOIN dbo.bank_tx_batch btb 
ON bt.batchId = btb.id
WHERE btb.iban = 'LU116060002000005040'
```
This comment can be removed with newest changes from develop.

Same question as last time 😉 I really like the strategy thinking ... but again there is a lot of duplicated code. E.g. the `sendToken` and `sendDFI` methods are practically the same. And it's not some trivial code but a very fundamental logic.
Is there a way to combine it? 😊
Why this `[...uniqueAddresses.entries()]`? (same for other occasions in this file)
I think you deleted the bank account thunder requests.
Let's talk to Oli for exact wording and translations.
I would solve this with different roles, e.g. `Wallet` and `KycWallet` or similar.
Should we move this unit parsing thingy into a helper method?

Perhaps we need to discuss this. From what I saw, BSC_CRYPTO is for native BNB and BSC_TOKEN for all BEP-20 tokens, is this right? Then the differentiation based on AssetCategory is not really fitting 🤔 
Same for other facades and Ethereum blockchain.
It seems to be very similar for all chains. There are the native asset and multiple wrapped tokens, which need to be handled differently.
Why not use dailyLimit/annualLimit as keys?
Should we remove these credentials? 😉  (same for challenge request)

body:
It might be better to put this into a map (on all exchange adapters), where all blockchains are required, e.g. 
```ts
const ccxtNetworks: { [b in Blockchain]: string } { ...}
```
I think it would make sense to add the address to this query.
How long does this approval take? What if the service restarts at this point?
Why are there two places for coin gas estimation in this class?
I would move this to ip-log service. Now, in the IP log the result would be false, even though the user was allowed.
And better use a new method on userService to get the user. `getUserByKey` is not typed and only for GS sync 😉
TODO: adapt to entity (remove unused, fix order)
Changes to `thunderEnvironment.json` should never be committed (access tokens!)
Changes to `thunderclient.json` should only be committed, if the changes are useful. In this case I would just revert it.
Divide by `10 ** 8` to get the BTC balance?
We normally use less "technical" variable/method names. In this case I would just use `const payments = await ...`. This makes it more readable IMO.

I would generally rather throw an error, if a required parameter is missing.
```ts
if (!description) throw new Error('Description is undefined');
```

Remove `supportedAssets` and get asset by
```ts
const asset = this.assetService.getLightningCoin();
```
Add TX ID also to on-ramp mail
Maybe store the space element (`{ key: '', style: '${MailDefaultStyle};padding:1px' }`) in a class variable?
Can this be simplified to the following? ```hbs {{this.key}}{{#if this.url}}{{this.url.text}}{{/if}} ```
Maybe you could also add an enum for special tags ...

Why no use the update endpoint (PUT)?
https://vm-dfx-btc-inp-loc.westeurope.cloudapp.azure.com:5000/docs#/lnurlp/api_link_create_or_update_lnurlp_api_v1_links__link_id__put

I wouldn't use this combine logic, but translation params. E.g.: ```ts `{ key: `${MailTranslationKey.RETURN}.introduction`, params: { reason: `${Util.parseMailKey(MailTranslationKey.RETURN, entity.amlReason)}` } }` ``` and in the translation file ```json "introduction": "Grund, warum wir zurückerstattet haben:
{reason}", ``` then translate ```ts this.tNew(element.key, lang, params); ``` where we should also translate all params.

body: Do we need this separated handling for personal mails?
Maybe rename `MailRequestInput` to `MailRequestUser` and add a new interface for `MailRequestPersonal`?
The try-catch doesn't make much sense here. Should be around lines 160-161 (and invalidate cache for all assets).
Use the AlchemyNetworkMapper (for `this.blockchain`)? Same for other chains.
Is this separated on purpose? Could be merged.
You could write a pagination helper method and reuse it in `getTokenBalancesByAlchemy` 😉
Add the blockchain to the log

I would return undefined, if input amount is undefined. E.g.
```ts
return xmrAmount && Util.round(xmrAmount * MoneroHelper.AU_XMR_FACTOR, 12);
```
The message `Monero Node not in sync` might be a bit confusing (everywhere), maybe it's completely down? 😉
For Monero, we only need to send transactions of `SendType.RETURN`, we will not forward the inputs.
We need to check if the send fee is too high (see e.g. Bitcoin strategy).
Just saw that the return-code is missing for Lightning as well.
We need to get the amount in BTC, and maybe USDT? 🤔  Maybe adapt the `isBtcChain` method.
Add payout order details to log (e.g. id)
try-catch is useless (already done in monero.service)
Maybe we should add a helper method for this?
Link text `hier` is a bit weird, isn't it?
More general message (`Klicke den nachfolgenden Link um dich bei DFX einzuloggen`)

body: Maybe we can move some logic to `src/shared/utils/test.util.ts`, it looks quite repetitive.
This looks very confusing. Maybe we should move the switch out of the for loop?
I think we can use find here
Maybe direction (`INCOMING`, `OUTGOING` and `BOTH`) would be easier to understand?
`async` can be removed, the filter should be in a `.then()` after the `Promise.all`
Can we allow expired prices (`.getPrice(feeAsset, this.chf, true)`) for CHF amounts everywhere?
Move this to `EvmStrategy` (is true for all EVM strategies)
Implement method `getMinConfirmations` in base strategy (`SendStrategy`), use `TransactionHelper`
Use method from base strategy (to be implemented)
`referenceFee` is a bit misleading. It's the fee in input asset.
It doesn't make sense to convert the `maxBlockchainFee` in this method, it's in CHF.

Hm, could we somehow use the EvmClient as a variable instead of extending it? The EvmClient has some important caching which is duplicated if we extend it.
Maybe remove the DEuroClient completely and move the logic to the DEuroService?
This will probably not work for DEuro? 😉

Throw `OrderFailedException`?
`OrderNotProcessableException` -> order currently not processable due to missing liquidity
`OrderFailedException` -> unknown or unrecoverable error
Should we just switch `dia` -> `mes` and keep the rest the same?

Should we also use an AsyncCache for tokens as we do for EVM?
Where does this number come from?
Just use `this.wallet.publicKey` instead of fetching whole `walletInfo`?
I think it should be a different address?
Does it make sense to look into multi-payout transactions? Maybe not because fees are low.
Maybe it should rather be `getForTokenChain` or something? It also fetches coin, not only token balance.
Why is the asset null, if it is a native transaction?
We could directly rename `ain` to `btc` (folder, module, ...)

Why not return it directly?
`return this.binancePayService.verifySignature(request.body, { timestamp, nonce, signature, certSN }); `?
You can use `payIn.address.address` instead of getting the address via account.
Can't you use `feeNativeAsset` instead of calculating the fee amount again?
This looks the same as below, maybe a helper method?
Is it possible to throw an error, if the transaction failed?
Can we find a more meaningful name for this method? What do we check?
Can we use Tatum SDK instead of these HTTP calls?
The + operator has higher precedence than the || operator. Are we missing quotes?
It could be interesting to create a map instead of searching the array 4 times.
Can we apply latest changes from evm.strategy here?
Why is it native fee + transfer fee?

How does this handle transactions that are still pending? This method should return false if the transaction is pending or doesn't have enough confirmations.
If it's no more pending but failed, it should throw an error.

I think we can simplify this by changing the method to `mapBinanceTransaction(result: WebhookResult, assets: Asset[]): PayInEntry[]` and doing something like this:
```ts
return paymentInstructions
   .map((pi) => ({
      senderAddresses: null,
      receiverAddress: null,
      txId: transactionId,
      txType: PayInType.PAYMENT,
      blockHeight: null,
      amount: pi.amount,
      asset: assets.find((a) => Util.equalsIgnoreCase(pi.currency, asset.name)),
   }))
   .filter((p) => p.asset);
```

Is it really required to have all this logic with access levels? Can't we just try to create a donation payment, if no key and no JWT are provided?
Currently, for donation payments, the payment link is checked twice 🤔
Can't we reuse the manual_check translations? Look the same 🤔
